source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: Semiconductors (public and private) are structurally advantaged because AI makes global GDP inexorably more silicon-intensive

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker's thesis is an explicit sector-level structural call — he says it is 'hard to bet against semiconductors public and private' as a category. He does not specifically name NVDA as a beneficiary of this particular thesis. The summary explicitly states 'No specific public company is singled out as the primary beneficiary.' Including NVDA is an inference about an obvious beneficiary, not a direct connection Baker made in this thesis.

Same reasoning as NVDA. Baker's thesis is a broad sector-level structural argument about silicon-intensity of GDP driven by AI. AVGO is not specifically named or connected to this exact thesis.
